judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports handle grouping, ordering, and the F-06 escalation equally well, but Report 2 clearly and mandatorily flags the shared RESULTS_DB collision risk (and states a private RESULTS_DB is required per engineer) while Report 1 only mentions it as an "optional" step without stating the collision/corruption risk explicitly, making Report 2 the stronger overall report.

report_1.evidence: Engineer C — registry config (`pipeline/registry.py`): F-02 ... F-05 ... F-09 ... Three one-line changes in one file; one small PR.

report_2.evidence: Batch B — pipeline/registry.py: F-02 (add aurora-1 to MODEL_ENDPOINTS), F-05 (bump DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION to rubric-v3), F-09 (point DEFAULT_DATASET at fixtures/eval_set.jsonl). Three one-line config fixes, one file, one owner.

report_1.evidence: hand out four workstreams, grouped strictly by file so no two engineers edit the same file ... Engineer B — rubric parsing (`pipeline/rubric.py`): F-01 ... plus its companion test F-11 ... Engineer C — registry config ... Ordering across groups: after triage, all four streams can run fully in parallel

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 runs Batches B, C, and D fully in parallel ... Batch A also starts in Wave 1 but its three-plus-one findings are sequenced internally by its single owner

report_1.evidence: F-06 — not an engineering decision. ... Changing (0.35, 0.65) silently re-scores every answer and breaks comparability with any historical results. This needs the reviewer/rubric owner to specify the new thresholds and the calibration evidence before anyone touches it

report_2.evidence: F-06 (tighten PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS in pipeline/score.py:3). This is not a bug fix; it is a scoring-policy change that alters every borderline score the pipeline produces ... It should be escalated to whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy for concrete thresholds and sign-off

report_1.evidence: Optionally set the RESULTS_DB env var — tests/conftest.py defaults it to /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db; engineers should point it at a scratch path rather than share that machine-global default.

report_2.evidence: tests/conftest.py points the suite at a machine-global path, /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db (via RESULTS_DB). Batches A, C, and the F-12/F-11 tests all run pytest; parallel runs on one machine would collide on that database unless each engineer sets a per-worktree RESULTS_DB.
